title: Chloé Zhao Shouts Out Fellow Filmmakers After 'Hamnet' Win For Best Drama At Golden Globes
text: Director Chloé Zhao's film Hamnet claimed the top prize for Best Film – Drama at the Golden Globes on Sunday night. The Focus Features production, based on Maggie O'Farrell's novel reimagining William Shakespeare's life, also earned Jessie Buckley the award for Best Female Actor in a Drama. Producer Steven Spielberg publicly endorsed the selection, stating he believed Zhao was uniquely suited to tell this particular story. After discovering the source material, Spielberg explained his reasoning for bringing the project to her. In her acceptance remarks, Zhao highlighted the cast and crew's vulnerability throughout production. She referenced comments from her lead actor Paul Mescal about the importance of allowing oneself to be authentically seen. Zhao also acknowledged fellow filmmaker Ryan Coogler, whose film Sinners competed in the same category. She praised the collective dedication of those in the industry, saying: I see so many of you have become so strong and tender at the same time and shared so much of yourselves. The other nominees in the drama category included Sentimental Value, It Was Just An Accident, and The Secret Agent.
